name: PolyFuzz (Multi-Method Fuzzy String Matching)

performance.notes: Depends on matcher: TF-IDF fast, BERT-based slow; vectorized for large lists

agent_readiness.notes: Multi-method fuzzy string matching framework. from polyfuzz import PolyFuzz. model = PolyFuzz('TF-IDF'). model.match(['Apple', 'Apples', 'Banana'], ['Mango', 'Apple', 'Bananas']). model.get_matches() → DataFrame with From, To, Similarity. Matchers: TF-IDF (default, fast), EditDistance (RapidFuzz), Embeddings (sentence-transformers). model = PolyFuzz(matchers=[TFIDF(), EditDistance(), Embeddings()]). model.group(link_min_similarity=0.75). model.visualize(). Best for matching lists of strings against reference lists. sklearn-style API.

gotchas: BERT-based Embeddings matcher requires sentence-transformers installed separately
Slower than RapidFuzz for simple edit-distance tasks
No streaming — loads all pairs in memory for similarity matrix
Not maintained as actively as alternatives
best_when: Matching two lists of strings (company names, product names) to find closest matches
When TF-IDF character n-gram matching gives better results than edit distance
Entity resolution across differently-formatted name lists
avoid_when: Real-time fuzzy search autocomplete (use typesense or meilisearch)
When you only need edit distance (rapidfuzz is faster)
summary: PolyFuzz matches two string lists using multiple methods simultaneously. PolyFuzz('TF-IDF').match(from_list, to_list).get_matches() → DataFrame. Matchers: TF-IDF (fast, default), EditDistance (RapidFuzz), Embeddings (sentence-transformers). model.group() for clustering. Visualize similarity. MIT by Maarten Grootendorst. Best for list-to-list entity matching comparing multiple methods. Slower than direct RapidFuzz for simple edit-distance. Not as actively maintained.
